highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.139: Agent View & /goal Command Released
highlights.summary: This release introduces the Agent View (Research Preview), accessible via `claude agents`, which shows all your Claude Code sessions — running, blocked, or completed — in one unified list. The new `/goal` command lets you set a completion condition and Claude will keep working autonomously until it's met. These two features together make long-running, multi-session workflows much easier to manage.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.141: terminalSequence Hooks & HTTPS Plugin Cloning
highlights.summary: The new `terminalSequence` field in hook JSON output allows hooks to emit desktop notifications, update window titles, and ring bells even when there's no controlling terminal — great for background or headless workflows. A new environment variable `CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_PREFER_HTTPS` lets users in SSH-restricted environments clone GitHub plugin sources over HTTPS instead. These are small but impactful quality-of-life improvements for advanced hook and plugin users.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest model in the Opus family, bringing new capabilities relevant to developers using Claude Code. Upgrading to a more powerful model can significantly improve the quality of code generation, refactoring, and reasoning in complex tasks. Developers using Claude Code should check the Anthropic blog for details on how to switch models in their workflow.

highlights.title: Show HN: adamsreview – Multi-Agent PR Reviews for Claude Code
highlights.summary: adamsreview is a Claude Code plugin that performs deeper pull request reviews using parallel sub-agents, validation passes, persistent JSON state, and optional ensemble reviews via Codex CLI. The creator reports it catches significantly more real bugs than Claude's built-in review capabilities. This is a practical example of how to extend Claude Code with custom multi-agent pipelines for everyday development tasks.

highlights.title: Show HN: I Built a 500K LOC Production App Alone in 7 Months
highlights.summary: A developer shares how they built a 500,000-line-of-code production application solo in 7 months using AI coding tools, with a 5-hour uncut live session as proof. This is a compelling real-world case study demonstrating the scale of what's possible when developers leverage AI assistants like Claude Code effectively. Beginners can watch the live session to see practical AI-assisted development workflows in action.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes (YouTube Tutorial)
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial offers a focused 30-minute walkthrough for developers who want to get productive with Claude Code quickly. It's a great middle-ground between quick-start guides and exhaustive courses, making it ideal for developers with some coding experience but new to Claude Code. Watch it alongside the VS Code extension to follow along hands-on.

highlights.title: Complete 60-Minute Claude Code Masterclass with Full Documentation (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: This Bilibili tutorial (in Chinese) provides a comprehensive 60-minute guide covering everything from installation to advanced Claude Code usage, with accompanying full documentation. It was produced over three weeks and is designed as a definitive reference for Chinese-speaking developers at all skill levels. If you prefer written references, the linked docs make it easy to review key concepts after watching.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.140: Smarter Agent Tool Matching & /goal Bug Fix
highlights.summary: Claude Code now accepts case- and separator-insensitive values for the Agent tool's `subagent_type` field, so inputs like `"Code Reviewer"` will correctly resolve to `code-reviewer` without errors. A bug where the `/goal` command silently hung when `disableAllHooks` or `allowManagedHooksOnly` was set has been fixed — it now shows a proper message instead. The updated agent color palette also makes it easier to visually distinguish between different agents in the UI.

tips_of_the_day: Use the new `claude agents` command to get a bird's-eye view of all your running and completed sessions — if you're juggling multiple tasks, this prevents duplicate work and helps you pick up exactly where you left off.

When writing hook scripts for Claude Code, take advantage of the `terminalSequence` field to send yourself a desktop notification when a long-running task finishes — this way you can context-switch freely without constantly checking back.

If you're in an environment where SSH is blocked (e.g., a corporate firewall), set the `CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_PREFER_HTTPS=true` environment variable before installing Claude Code plugins to avoid connection errors when cloning from GitHub.
